title: Mission
summary: Why this exists and how content stays honest.
body:
Why this exists
One place for AEM job search in India — companies, apply steps, and interview prep.

  • Only add what can be backed up with a link
  • Keep it useful long-term, not just for one job hunt
  • Write clearly — no filler
  • Fix and improve as more is learned

title: Career Strategy
summary: Where AEM jobs show up in India and how to pick companies.
body: In India, AEM roles show up at product companies, GCCs, and agencies / SIs — each is different in pace, ownership, and interviews. Use Companies to shortlist, then Resume & LinkedIn templates to prep your profile, then How to Apply for day-to-day steps. Go deep on AEM (Sites, Cloud Service, Dispatcher, integrations) instead of claiming "full-stack everything." Even when you're not switching jobs, stay ready: keep LinkedIn/Naukri updated, do a low-pressure interview every few months, and fix whatever gaps show up. This isn't a ranking — every company has trade-offs.

title: Resume & LinkedIn templates
summary: Copy-paste patterns for resume, LinkedIn, Naukri, and outreach.
body: Present yourself as an AEM Developer with clear skills in Sites, Cloud Service, Dispatcher, and integrations. Fill in your real projects and numbers — don't make up impact. Templates below are for LinkedIn, Naukri, outreach messages, and resume bullets.

title: Core Skills
summary: AEM technologies worth knowing, with doc links.
body: Start with the basics (Sites, Sling, HTL, OSGi, Dispatcher, Java for AEM), then move to Cloud Service, headless, and Experience Platform hooks. The table links to official Adobe and Apache docs — use those instead of random blog posts when you're studying.

title: Learning Roadmap
summary: Step-by-step AEM topics with doc links.
body: New or rusty? Start with AEM basics (architecture → Sling/HTL → Dispatcher). Next: Cloud Service and Edge. When you need personalization / data: Experience Platform. Each step has Adobe / Apache doc links — go at your own speed.

title: Companies
summary: ~298 employers that hires for AEM — filter by India, cloud, and more.
body: Looking for employers not listed here? BuiltWith lists sites detected on AEM — AEM websitelist or AEM trends. Use as a discovery hint, then confirm hiring on careers pages and job boards.

title: How to Apply
summary: Resume, LinkedIn, Naukri, referrals, and notice period — step by step.
body: Practical steps for staying job-ready on Indian portals — not only during active search. Covers notice period, outreach, and learning from every interview. Resume templates are in Resume & LinkedIn templates above.

title: Community
summary: LinkedIn groups for AEM openings and tips.
body: LinkedIn groups where people share openings and tips. They're independent — not run by this site, Adobe, or employers listed here. Follow each group's rules; don't spam resumes. Use alongside Companies and your own LinkedIn/Naukri search.

title: Career tracker (planned)
summary: Possible future trackers for applications and interviews.
body:
• Company list ✓
  • Recruiter tracker (planned)
  • Application tracker (planned)
  • Interview tracker (planned)
  • Learning tracker (roadmaps + glossary live)
  • Decision log

title: Glossary
summary: Short definitions for AEM and DXP terms used here.
body: Quick definitions for terms you'll see in interviews and roadmaps. For full detail, use Adobe Experience League and Apache docs.

title: Interview Prep
summary: Technical and behavioural questions to practice.
body: Interviews depend on what you know, what's on your resume, and how the interviewer runs the loop — basics like JCR, Java, Sling, OSGi, Dispatcher, HTL, and ResourceResolver come up a lot. Use the list below as a checklist, not a script; adapt to your experience. Real project work you can explain beats memorising AI-generated answers — but still know the fundamentals.

title: About this data
summary: What's researched here and what you should double-check yourself.
body: Research notes shared with other AEM developers. Use as a starting point — always confirm openings on employer careers pages and job boards.

title: Doc links
summary: Official Adobe docs and courses referenced across the site.
body: Adobe documentation and courses linked from Learning Roadmap and Core Skills.
